summary: Always call `db.commit()` before writing to Redis in functions that persist a DB row then cache it — if the commit fails, the Redis entry must not exist or it will expose a version that was never persisted. Also clear any process-level date-keyed dicts (`_foo_cache.clear()`) immediately after a successful commit so that in-process cache entries for stale data are evicted on retrain. See `regime_service.train_and_persist`.
